items.title: VI Live: Summerville scoort tegen Chelsea, PEC slaat toe tegen Telstar
items.summary: Welkom in de VI Live van zaterdag 31 januari. Er staan drie duels in de Eredivisie op het programma, Liverpool krijgt bezoek van Newcastle United en de regerend kampioenen van Spanje, Duitsland en Italië komen in actie. Volg alle hoogtepunten in de VI Live.

items.title: Chelsea v West Ham United: Premier League – live
items.summary: ⚽️ Premier League updates from the 5.30pm (GMT) kick-off ⚽️ Live scores | Full table | Follow on Bluesky | Email Billy Enzo Fernández and Jarrod Bowen lead the teams out. Anthony Taylor is the man in the middle. We’re ready to go. Warm-ups are done at the Bridge. Chelsea were harking back to the 90s with their training strip based on that notorious away kit. Continue reading...

items.title: Positief blessurenieuws over Shaqueel van Persie: mogelijk nog minuten dit seizoen
items.summary: Feyenoord heeft een positieve medische update geplaatst over Shaqueel van Persie. Gevreesd werd voor een zware knieblessure nadat de zoon van trainer Robin van Persie geblesseerd was uitgevallen in het Europa League-duel met Real Betis, maar mogelijk maakt Van Persie junior dit seizoen nog minuten.

items.title: Wereldgoal Sano hoogtepunt van masterclass NEC in Alkmaar
items.summary: NEC heeft zaterdag indruk gemaakt door AZ in het eigen AFAS Stadion te overklassen. De ploeg van interim-trainer Leeroy Echteld was in grote delen van de Eredivisie-wedstrijd machteloos tegen de mannen van Dick Schreuder, die nu al elf wedstrijden ongeslagen zijn.

items.title: AZ volledig afgetroefd: 'Zoals NEC speelt, willen wij spelen'
items.summary: Leeroy Echteld erkent dat NEC zaterdag simpelweg de betere ploeg was. AZ werd op eigen veld overklast door de Nijmeegse bezoekers en met name Alexandre Penetra had het lastig als rechtsback. Toch heeft de interim-trainer geen spijt van de beslissing om de Portugese verdediger op die positie te zetten.

items.title: 'I've not got a crystal ball' - Edwards coy on Strand Larsen future
items.summary: Wolves manager Rob Edwards says that recruitment for his side has been difficult in January, whilst adding he is unsure about the future of striker Jorgen Strand Larsen.

items.title: Arsenal kijkt vooruit naar 'vier lange maanden'
items.summary: Na driemaal puntenverlies op rij in de Premier League, won Arsenal zaterdag overtuigend van Leeds United . De koploper is trots op het resultaat, maar weet dat het seizoen nog lang is.

items.title: Snoop Dogg wants to make Swansea 'global name'
items.summary: Swansea City investor Snoop Dogg has said he wants to take the side back to the Premier League, and establish them as a 'global name'.

items.title: Arbeloa wants Madrid fan support after UCL blow
items.summary: Real Madrid coach Alvaro Arbeloa said he doesn't expect the team's fans to voice their disapproval as they return to LaLiga action on Sunday

items.title: Arsenal thrash Leeds to reinstate 7-point PL lead
items.summary: Arsenal have reinstated their seven-point lead at the top of the Premier League after a 4-0 win against Leeds United.

items.title: Messi, CR7 jerseys headline Terry's $695K auction
items.summary: Former England and Chelsea captain John Terry netted $695,000 from a 50-item auction of his personal collection, headlined by match-worn Lionel Messi and Cristiano Ronaldo jerseys.

items.title: Wrexham boost PL promotion hopes with 1-0 win
items.summary: Wrexham moved back into the Championship play-off places after their hard- fought 1-0 win over Sheffield Wednesday at Hillsborough.

items.title: Arsenal showed character and quality in win at Leeds - Arteta
items.summary: Arsenal manager Mikel Arteta says he is pleased with the dominance, character and quality that his side showed in their 4-0 win over Leeds.

items.title: Championship roundup: QPR comeback stuns Coventry as Boro move level with win
items.summary: Middlesbrough edge Norwich, Hull win at Blackburn Ipswich grab late draw, Charlton add to Leicester woes Stuttering Coventry’s grip at the top of the Championship loosened further as QPR came from behind to beat them 2-1 at Loftus Road. Josh Eccles gave the Sky Blues an early lead but Rangers responded in the second half with Richard Kone’s equaliser and Nicolas Madsen’s winner. Frank Lampard’s side’s are now level on points with second-placed Middlesbrough, who took advantage by ending Norwich’s winning run with a 1-0 victory at the Riverside. Continue reading...

items.title: Iraola hails 'incredible' goalscoring teenager Kroupi
items.summary: Bournemouth manager Andoni Iraola credits much of his side's recent improvment to "incredible" performances of Eli Junior Kroupi, as well as the depth brought in by new signings.

items.title: Philadelphia's George banned for 25 NBA games after anti-drug violation
items.summary: Philadelphia 76ers forward Paul George has been suspended for 25 games without pay for violating anti-drug protocol, the NBA announced on Saturday.

items.title: Beto snatches last-gasp equaliser for Everton to spark Moyes sprint and frustrate Brighton
items.summary: David Moyes could not contain himself. As the ball hit the back of the net after the substitute Beto had bundled in the equaliser in the seventh minute of injury time, the Everton manager set off. Moyes is 63 in April, but he sprinted on to the pitch in a moment of sheer delight as Brighton’s players dropped to their knees in despair. Somehow, Everton’s unbeaten record here that stretches back to 2019 is still intact. It had seemed a goal from Pascal Gross would be enough for Brighton to claim victory at the sixth attempt after they had spurned a number of chances in the first half. But while it is one thing to plan how you are going to overcome a team managed by Moyes, it is not quite so easy in practice. Continue reading...

items.title: Kilmarnock end 17-game winless run in dominant victory over Aberdeen
items.summary: Kilmarnock finally ended a horror 17-game winless run with a dominant 3-0 victory against 10-man Aberdeen at Rugby Park.

items.title: Arsenal stroll back to winning ways against outclassed Leeds and go seven points clear
items.summary: It would be a touch too hyperbolic to suggest this was a season-defining afternoon for Arsenal’s title ambitions, but given the opposition and the pre-match drama surrounding this game, there was no doubting that come full time this felt like a significant afternoon in the Premier League title picture. Two points from three games is hardly compelling enough evidence to prompt full-blown crisis talks, but given the lofty standards Arsenal have set in the first half of this season, it felt as though we would learn plenty about them here . This against a team who have lost once since the start of December and with a ferocious home crowd behind them. Continue reading...

items.title: Bournemouth build on Liverpool win as Kroupi and Scott sink Wolves
items.summary: Two clubs in the process of a reset. While Wolves’ fate is all but sealed, they seek to carry good vibes into the Championship. Bournemouth’s objective was to make last Saturday’s defeat of Liverpool the staging post for one of those streaks of good results that have made Andoni Iraola’s reputation. Mission accomplished for the Cherries, who could celebrate their second away win of a troubled season, a first since August. In the performance of the debutant Rayan, a second-half sub, who supplied Alex Scott’s late clincher, there is much to look forward to. Continue reading...
